data.content:
### POST /admin/generate_link Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md This endpoint generates an email action link based on the specified type, such as signup, magiclink, recovery, or invite. It requires admin authorization and includes details like the action link, email OTP, hashed token, verification type, and redirect URL. The response provides query parameters separately for convenience along with the email OTP and hashed token. ```APIDOC ## POST /admin/generate_link ### Description Generates an email action link based on the type (signup, magiclink, recovery, or invite) and returns the link along with query parameters, email OTP, hashed token, and verification details. Requires admin authorization. ### Method POST ### Endpoint /admin/generate_link ### Parameters #### Path Parameters None #### Query Parameters None #### Request Body - **type** (string) - Required - The type of link to generate: 'signup', 'magiclink', 'recovery', or 'invite'. - **email** (string) - Required - The email address for the link. - **password** (string) - Optional - User's password, required if type is 'signup'. - **data** (object) - Optional - Additional signup data, required if type is 'signup'. - **redirect_to** (string) - Optional - Redirect URL after email action, defaults to SITE_URL. ### Request Example { "type": "signup", "email": "email@example.com", "password": "secret", "data": { ... }, "redirect_to": "https://supabase.io" } ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **action_link** (string) - The full action link URL. - **email_otp** (string) - The email OTP code. - **hashed_token** (string) - The hashed token. - **verification_type** (string) - The type of verification. - **redirect_to** (string) - The redirect URL after action. #### Response Example { "action_link": "http://localhost:9999/verify?token=TOKEN&type=TYPE&redirect_to=REDIRECT_URL", "email_otp": "EMAIL_OTP", "hashed_token": "TOKEN", "verification_type": "TYPE", "redirect_to": "REDIRECT_URL", ... } ``` -------------------------------- ### POST /admin/generate_link Endpoint in JavaScript Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md Generates email action links for types like signup, magiclink, recovery, or invite. Requires admin JWT authorization. Body specifies type, email, optional password/data, and redirect_to URL; defaults to SITE_URL. Returns action_link, email_otp, hashed_token, and verification details. ```javascript headers: { "Authorization": "Bearer eyJhbGciOiJI...M3A90LCkxxtX9oNP9KZO" // admin role required } body: { "type": "signup" or "magiclink" or "recovery" or "invite", "email": "email@example.com", "password": "secret", // only if type = signup "data": { ... }, // only if type = signup "redirect_to": "https://supabase.io" // Redirect URL to send the user to after an email action. Defaults to SITE_URL. } Returns { "action_link": "http://localhost:9999/verify?token=TOKEN&type=TYPE&redirect_to=REDIRECT_URL", "email_otp": "EMAIL_OTP", "hashed_token": "TOKEN", "verification_type": "TYPE", "redirect_to": "REDIRECT_URL", ... } ``` -------------------------------- ### POST/PUT /admin/users Admin User Management in JavaScript Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md Creates or updates a user via admin endpoint using POST or PUT. Requires JWT with admin role from GOTRUE_JWT_ADMIN_ROLES. Body includes user details like email, phone, metadata; ban_duration uses time units like '24h' or 'none' to unban. Limitations: password only for signup type. ```javascript headers: { "Authorization": "Bearer eyJhbGciOiJI...M3A90LCkxxtX9oNP9KZO" // requires a role claim that can be set in the GOTRUE_JWT_ADMIN_ROLES env var } body: { "role": "test-user", "email": "email@example.com", "phone": "12345678", "password": "secret", // only if type = signup "email_confirm": true, "phone_confirm": true, "user_metadata": {}, "app_metadata": {}, "ban_duration": "24h" or "none" // to unban a user } ``` -------------------------------- ### POST /admin/users/ Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md This endpoint creates a new user with the specified user_id if using POST, or updates an existing user if using PUT. It requires admin authorization and allows setting user details such as role, email, phone, password, confirmations, and metadata. The ban_duration can be used to ban or unban the user with time units like '24h'. ```APIDOC ## POST /admin/users/ ### Description Creates a new user with the specified user_id using POST or updates an existing user using PUT. Requires admin authorization and supports setting various user attributes including role, contact info, and ban duration. ### Method POST ### Endpoint /admin/users/ ### Parameters #### Path Parameters - **user_id** (string) - Required - The ID of the user to create or update. #### Query Parameters None #### Request Body - **role** (string) - Optional - The role for the user. - **email** (string) - Optional - User's email address. - **phone** (string) - Optional - User's phone number. - **password** (string) - Optional - User's password, required for signup type. - **email_confirm** (boolean) - Optional - Whether email is confirmed. - **phone_confirm** (boolean) - Optional - Whether phone is confirmed. - **user_metadata** (object) - Optional - Additional user metadata. - **app_metadata** (object) - Optional - Application-specific metadata. - **ban_duration** (string) - Optional - Ban duration in time format (e.g., '24h') or 'none' to unban. ### Request Example { "role": "test-user", "email": "email@example.com", "phone": "12345678", "password": "secret", "email_confirm": true, "phone_confirm": true, "user_metadata": {}, "app_metadata": {}, "ban_duration": "24h" } ### Response #### Success Response (200 or 201) - Response details not explicitly provided in the input, assuming success with no specific fields. #### Response Example Response body not specified in input. ``` -------------------------------- ### PUT /admin/users/ Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md This endpoint updates an existing user based on the specified user_id using PUT. Similar to POST but for updates, it requires admin authorization and allows modifying user attributes such as role, email, phone, confirmations, metadata, and ban status. The ban_duration accepts time units for temporary bans or 'none' to unban. ```APIDOC ## PUT /admin/users/ ### Description Updates an existing user with the specified user_id. Requires admin authorization and allows modifying user details, confirmations, metadata, and ban settings. ### Method PUT ### Endpoint /admin/users/ ### Parameters #### Path Parameters - **user_id** (string) - Required - The ID of the user to update. #### Query Parameters None #### Request Body - **role** (string) - Optional - The role for the user. - **email** (string) - Optional - User's email address. - **phone** (string) - Optional - User's phone number. - **password** (string) - Optional - User's password, required for signup type. - **email_confirm** (boolean) - Optional - Whether email is confirmed. - **phone_confirm** (boolean) - Optional - Whether phone is confirmed. - **user_metadata** (object) - Optional - Additional user metadata. - **app_metadata** (object) - Optional - Application-specific metadata. - **ban_duration** (string) - Optional - Ban duration in time format (e.g., '24h') or 'none' to unban. ### Request Example { "role": "test-user", "email": "email@example.com", "phone": "12345678", "password": "secret", "email_confirm": true, "phone_confirm": true, "user_metadata": {}, "app_metadata": {}, "ban_duration": "24h" } ### Response #### Success Response (200) - Response details not explicitly provided in the input, assuming success with no specific fields. #### Response Example Response body not specified in input. ``` -------------------------------- ### Configure SMTP Settings for Supabase Auth Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md Sets up SMTP server configuration for sending emails in Supabase Auth. Requires host, port, user, password, admin email, and subject. Inputs are environment variables for mail server details. Outputs enable email functionality for auth features like confirmations. ```properties GOTRUE_SMTP_HOST=smtp.mandrillapp.com GOTRUE_SMTP_PORT=587 GOTRUE_SMTP_USER=smtp-delivery@example.com GOTRUE_SMTP_PASS=correcthorsebatterystaple GOTRUE_SMTP_ADMIN_EMAIL=support@example.com GOTRUE_MAILER_SUBJECTS_CONFIRMATION="Please confirm" ``` -------------------------------- ### POST /magiclink Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md Sends a magic link to the user's email address, which can be used to redeem an access token. Note: It's recommended to use `/otp` instead. Rate limited to once every 60 seconds. ```APIDOC ## POST /magiclink ### Description Sends a magic link to the user's email address for authentication. ### Method POST ### Endpoint /magiclink ### Parameters #### Request Body - **email** (string) - Required - The email address of the user. ### Request Example ```json { "email": "email@example.com" } ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) Returns an empty JSON object upon successful request. #### Response Example ```json {} ``` **Note**: The magic link, when clicked, redirects to `#access_token=x&refresh_token=y&expires_in=z&token_type=bearer&type=magiclink`. ``` -------------------------------- ### Send Magic Link - JSON Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md This endpoint delivers a magic link to the user's email address, allowing them to redeem an access token. It's recommended to use /otp instead for improved security. The input is a JSON object containing the user's email address. The endpoint returns an empty response. ```json { "email": "email@example.com" } ``` -------------------------------- ### Email Template for Magic Link Login in Supabase Auth Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md HTML template for magic link emails. Uses template variables like SiteURL, Email, and ConfirmationURL. Displays a link to log in via magic link. Falls back to this default if custom template is unavailable. ```html

Magic Link

Follow this link to login:

Log In

``` -------------------------------- ### POST /otp Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md One-Time-Password delivery via magic link or SMS OTP. Can create user if not exists. ```APIDOC ## POST /otp ### Description One-Time-Password delivery via magic link or SMS OTP. Can create user if not exists. ### Method POST ### Endpoint /otp ### Parameters #### Request Body - **phone** (string) - Optional - User's phone number in E.164 format - **email** (string) - Optional - User's email address - **create_user** (boolean) - Optional - Whether to create user if not exists (default: true) ### Request Example ```json { "phone": "12345678", "create_user": true } ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) Empty response #### Response Example ```json {} ``` ``` -------------------------------- ### PUT /user Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md Updates a user's profile information, including email, password, phone, and custom data. Changing email triggers a magic link email. Requires authentication. ```APIDOC ## PUT /user ### Description Updates the profile of the currently authenticated user. This can include changing email, password, phone number, or setting custom user data. ### Method PUT ### Endpoint /user ### Parameters Requires authentication via the `Authorization` header. #### Request Body - **email** (string) - Optional - The new email address for the user. Changing this will send a verification email. - **password** (string) - Optional - The new password for the user. - **phone** (string) - Optional - The new phone number for the user. - **data** (object) - Optional - A JSON object to store custom user data. - **key** (any) - Example custom data field. - **number** (integer) - Example custom data field. - **admin** (boolean) - Example custom data field. ### Request Example ```json { "email": "new-email@example.com", "password": "new-password", "phone": "+123456789", "data": { "key": "value", "number": 10, "admin": false } } ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **id** (string) - The user's ID. - **email** (string) - The user's email. - **email_change_sent_at** (string) - Timestamp when the email change verification was sent. - **phone** (string) - The user's phone number. - **phone_change_sent_at** (string) - Timestamp when the phone change verification was sent. - **created_at** (string) - Timestamp when the user account was created. - **updated_at** (string) - Timestamp when the user account was last updated. #### Response Example ```json { "id": "11111111-2222-3333-4444-5555555555555", "email": "email@example.com", "email_change_sent_at": "2016-05-15T20:49:40.882805774-07:00", "phone": "+123456789", "phone_change_sent_at": "2016-05-15T20:49:40.882805774-07:00", "created_at": "2016-05-15T19:53:12.368652374-07:00", "updated_at": "2016-05-15T19:53:12.368652374-07:00" } ``` **Security Note**: If `GOTRUE_SECURITY_UPDATE_PASSWORD_REQUIRE_REAUTHENTICATION` is enabled, reauthentication with a nonce will be required before updating the password. The request body would then include `password` and `nonce`. ``` -------------------------------- ### GET /verify Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md Verify a registration or password recovery via query params. User will be logged in and redirected. ```APIDOC ## GET /verify ### Description Verify a registration or a password recovery via query params. User will be logged in and redirected. ### Method GET ### Endpoint /verify ### Parameters #### Query Parameters - **type** (string) - Required - Type of verification (signup, recovery, magiclink, invite) - **token** (string) - Required - Token from /signup, /recover, or /magiclink - **redirect_to** (string) - Optional - URL to redirect after verification ### Request Example ```json { "type": "signup", "token": "confirmation-code-delivered-in-email", "redirect_to": "https://supabase.io" } ``` ### Response User will be redirected to: ``` SITE_URL/#access_token=jwt-token-representing-the-user&token_type=bearer&expires_in=3600&refresh_token=a-refresh-token&type=invite ``` ``` -------------------------------- ### POST /verify Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md Verify a registration or password recovery. Type can be signup, recovery, invite, or sms. ```APIDOC ## POST /verify ### Description Verify a registration or a password recovery. Type can be `signup`, `recovery`, `invite`, or `sms`. ### Method POST ### Endpoint /verify ### Parameters #### Request Body - **type** (string) - Required - Type of verification (signup, recovery, invite, sms) - **token** (string) - Required - Token from /signup or /recover - **password** (string) - Optional - User's password (required for signup verification if no existing password) - **redirect_to** (string) - Optional - URL to redirect after verification - **phone** (string) - Optional - Phone number SMS OTP was delivered to ### Request Example ```json { "type": "signup", "token": "confirmation-code-delivered-in-email" } ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **access_token** (string) - JWT token representing the user - **token_type** (string) - Type of token (bearer) - **expires_in** (number) - Token expiration time in seconds - **refresh_token** (string) - Refresh token - **type** (string) - Type of verification (signup, recovery, invite) #### Response Example ```json { "access_token": "jwt-token-representing-the-user", "token_type": "bearer", "expires_in": 3600, "refresh_token": "a-refresh-token", "type": "signup" } ``` ``` -------------------------------- ### Identity Unlinked Notification Template Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md HTML template for notifying users when an identity provider has been unlinked from their account, with Email and Provider variables. ```html

An identity has been unlinked

An identity ({{ .Provider }}) has been unlinked from your account {{ .Email }}.

If you did not make this change, please contact support immediately.

``` -------------------------------- ### Identity Linked Notification Template Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md HTML template for notifying users when a new identity provider has been linked to their account, with Email and Provider variables for details. ```html

A new identity has been linked

A new identity ({{ .Provider }}) has been linked to your account {{ .Email }}.

If you did not make this change, please contact support immediately.

``` -------------------------------- ### Email Template for Signup Confirmation in Supabase Auth Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md HTML template for signup confirmation emails. Uses template variables like SiteURL, Email, and ConfirmationURL. Displays a link to confirm the user account. Falls back to this default if custom template is unavailable. ```html

Confirm your signup

Follow this link to confirm your user:

Confirm your mail

``` -------------------------------- ### GET /settings Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/README.md This endpoint retrieves the publicly available settings for the auth instance, including external provider statuses and signup configurations. It allows clients to dynamically check which authentication providers are enabled and if signup is disabled or autoconfirmation is set. The response provides a JSON object with boolean flags for each provider. ```APIDOC ## GET /settings ### Description Retrieves the publicly available settings for this auth instance, including status of external providers and signup configurations. ### Method GET ### Endpoint /settings ### Parameters #### Path Parameters None #### Query Parameters None #### Request Body None ### Request Example No request body required. ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **external** (object) - Object containing boolean flags for each external provider (e.g., apple, azure, bitbucket, etc.) - **disable_signup** (boolean) - Indicates if signup is disabled - **autoconfirm** (boolean) - Indicates if email confirmation is automatic #### Response Example { "external": { "apple": true, "azure": true, "bitbucket": true, "discord": true, "facebook": true, "figma": true, "github": true, "gitlab": true, "google": true, "keycloak": true, "linkedin": true, "notion": true, "slack": true, "snapchat": true, "spotify": true, "twitch": true, "twitter": true, "workos": true }, "disable_signup": false, "autoconfirm": false } ``` -------------------------------- ### POST /admin/users Source: https://github.com/supabase/auth/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md Creates a new user in the Supabase Auth system. Requires a JWT with 'supabase_admin' role. ```APIDOC ## POST /admin/users ### Description Creates a new user by sending an email and password in the request body. Requires an Authorization header with a Bearer token. ### Method POST ### Endpoint /admin/users ### Parameters #### Request Body - **email** (string) - Required - The email address of the user to create. - **password** (string) - Required - The password for the new user. ### Request Example ```json { "email": "user@example.com", "password": "12345678" } ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **id** (string) - The unique identifier for the created user. - **aud** (string) - The audience of the token. - **role** (string) - The role assigned to the user. - **email** (string) - The email address of the user. - **phone** (string) - The phone number of the user. - **app_metadata** (object) - Application-specific metadata. - **user_metadata** (object) - User-defined metadata. - **identities** (array) - List of user identities. - **created_at** (string) - Timestamp when the user was created. - **updated_at** (string) - Timestamp when the user was last updated. #### Response Example ```json { "id": "e78c512d-68e4-482b-901b-75003e89acae", "aud": "authenticated", "role": "authenticated", "email": "user@example.com", "phone": "", "app_metadata": { "provider": "email", "providers": ["email"] }, "user_metadata": {}, "identities": null, "created_at": "2021-12-15T12:40:03.507551-05:00", "updated_at": "2021-12-15T12:40:03.512067-05:00" } ``` ```
